comments.author: Jesse Tharp
comments.text: Evaluation of PDF extraction libraries complete. Benchmarked pdfplumber, PyMuPDF (fitz), and pymupdf4llm against pdfminer.six. Result: pdfplumber is the winner for accuracy and domain-entity alignment. It provides excellent word-level coherence and font metadata needed for footnote/verse separation. Speed: ~0.8s/page (2x faster than current). Detailed report saved to docs/pdf_extraction_evaluation.md. Recommendation: Refactor NoabPdfSource to use pdfplumber.

comments.author: Jesse Tharp
comments.text:
FULL REFACTOR WALKTHROUGH

Key Improvements:
1. Superior Layout Analysis
   - Column-Major Sorting: Fixed text interleaving between columns.
   - Drop-Cap Support: Recovered 'I' in 'In the beginning'.

2. OCR-Assisted Data Recovery

3. Discrimination Logic
   - Epistle/Gospel Separation: Resolved John collisions via word boundaries.
   - Intro-Text Filtering: Excluded sz 7.2 prologue text from verses.

Verification Results:

- Genesis 1:1 restored with 'IN THE BEGINNING'.
- All 11 sample companions verified sequentially.

Status: READY FOR SIGN-OFF.

notes:
SESSION CONTEXT (2026-03-30): Major research session. No code changes yet — all planning and documentation.

ARCHITECTURE STATUS:
- pdfplumber + ordered-box parsing confirmed correct (Codex session)
- Genesis 1: 31/31 verses passing (real regression)
- Genesis 2: 13/25, John 1: 15/51, Romans 8: 10/39 — still blocked

LINEAR CHANGES THIS SESSION:
- New milestone 'Phase 4 — Photocopy PDF Sources' created
- Old 'Phase 4 — Interlinear Hubs' renamed to Phase 5
- PER-8 fully updated with Codex findings, screenshot analysis, Anthony OCR results
- PER-25 created: LLM auditor quality gate framework
- Both issues have comments with full session context

KEY FINDINGS FROM COLLEAGUE (Anthony Cairo — deed-book project):
1. Pipe-delimiter zone detection: his --format rows output uses | to separate columns. First row with | = intro ends, verse zone begins. Last | row = verse zone ends. Clean content-driven boundary signal, solves variable-zone-boundary problem.
2. GlyphLessFont is image-transparent: corruption exists only in the PDF text layer. Image OCR sees correct digits. Targeted image OCR on unresolved markers is the right fix path.
3. Mid-line verse numbers still hard: his tool also missed v4 in Genesis 1 (inline mid-paragraph). Not unique to our approach.
4. Custom OCR binary incoming: Anthony built a custom binary after Tesseract failures. Pending receipt — may change OCR engine choice for PER-25.

RECOMMENDED HYBRID APPROACH (documented in PER-8):
1. Image OCR rows format → detect verse zone boundaries (pipe delimiter)
2. pdfplumber word-level within that zone → verse/marker extraction with font metadata
3. Targeted image OCR crop → fallback for GlyphLessFont markers pdfplumber can't recover

NEXT STEPS (when resuming NOAB work):
1. Wait for Anthony's custom binary — evaluate before next adapter pass
2. Probe pdfplumber page.lines/page.rects for horizontal rule detectability (may give even cleaner zone boundaries than OCR)
3. Add target regressions for John 1:1 and Romans 8:1-4
4. Improve implicit verse-1 start detection for shared pages
